task_completion.description: Verify the 'Mandible_Demo.bsp' project is opened in Blue Sky Plan.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Blue Sky Plan application window is open, and the project name 'Mandible_Demo' is visible in the title bar, project panel, or active workspace.

task_completion.description: Verify the software view is focused on the lower right canine and first premolar area.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 2D slice (axial/coronal/sagittal) or 3D view is zoomed in on the lower right quadrant of the mandible, specifically showing the canine and first premolar.

task_completion.description: Verify a horizontal measurement is taken between the roots at the mid-root level.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A measurement line (using the software's distance/ruler tool) is drawn horizontally between the roots of the lower right canine and first premolar, positioned approximately mid-root (5mm below the crest of the bone). The distance value should be visible on screen.

task_completion.description: Verify a screenshot of the measurement view was captured.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A screenshot tool is actively capturing the screen, or a saved image file is visible, clearly displaying the roots and the measurement line from the Blue Sky Plan software.

task_completion.description: Verify the findings are documented in 'tad_safety_report.txt' with the required format.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor is open showing the file 'C:\Users\Docker\Documents\tad_safety_report.txt'. The text contains exactly 'Measurement: [X.X] mm' and 'Conclusion: [Safe/Risky]'.

integrity.description: Verify the agent interacted with the actual Blue Sky Plan GUI to perform the measurement.
integrity.visual_evidence: The measurement tool is actively used within the Blue Sky Plan interface. The agent did not bypass the software by faking an image or using terminal commands to generate the report without measuring.

integrity.description: Verify the measurement value in the report matches the software measurement.
integrity.visual_evidence: The 'X.X mm' value typed into the text report exactly matches the distance value displayed on the measurement line in the Blue Sky Plan screenshot.

integrity.description: Verify the conclusion strictly follows the threshold logic based on the measurement.
integrity.visual_evidence: The report states 'Safe' ONLY if the recorded measurement is strictly greater than 3.0mm, and 'Risky' ONLY if it is 3.0mm or less. It should not be a random guess.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Blue Sky Plan (v5.0) is a real FDA-cleared dental planning software. The 3.0 mm safety threshold for TAD placement is clinically accurate. CRITICAL: The task description contains a factual tooth numbering error. It identifies the lower right canine and first premolar as #27 and #28. In FDI notation, #27 and #28 are upper left teeth (the correct lower right teeth are #43 and #44). The VLM should ensure the agent targets the anatomical lower right canine and first premolar, despite the numbering confusion in the prompt.
